commentary: Portfolio positioned well to benefit from AI capex resilience despite inflation headwinds. Tech concentration (NVIDIA, Apple, Alphabet, Microsoft, Intel, Amazon ~26% combined) offers upside if geopolitical tensions ease and Fed policy stabilizes under Warsh. Luxury positions (LVMH, Hermès) face near-term pressure from China demand uncertainty; recovery dependent on 2H 2026 consumer rebound and Middle East de-escalation. Mastercard positioned for 2H recovery as cross-border volumes normalize. Key risk: Warsh Fed chair confirmation vote today (May 13) could clarify inflation-vs-growth policy stance; valuations compressed if hawkish surprise emerges.

tickers.thesis: CEO's China summit participation signals regulatory thaw; combined with $725B+ hyperscaler capex spend, NVIDIA positioned for earnings beat and forward guidance raise. Geopolitical recovery narrative intact despite inflation surprise.
key_drivers: Q1 earnings beat on hyperscaler capex (May 22)

tickers.thesis: Intel partnership reduces TSMC supply risk but unlikely to materially boost margins near-term. WWDC June 4 keynote offers product upgrade catalyst. China demand trajectory remains key—geopolitical thaw supportive but consumer confidence still fragile.

Q2 guidance expected to show near-term pressure from cross-border slowdown due to Iran war premium, but 2H recovery narrative intact. Valuation compression creates attractive entry for patient capital; 90% analyst bullish consensus supports gradual upside into 2H.

<cite index="5-6,5-7">Investors were most enthusiastic about the results of Google parent Alphabet, whose shares climbed as much as 6% in after-hours trading. The company reported earnings and revenue that beat analysts' expectations and raised its estimate of how much it would spend on AI infrastructure.</cite> Positioned for steady gains on capex visibility and search stability.

<cite index="23-4">The luxury powerhouse lost ground in January, February, March, and April, resulting in a 30% wipeout of its market capitalization so far this year.</cite> Near-term headwinds persist; <cite index="23-14">LVMH is trading at 17 times expected 2028 earnings, a level never seen in the last ten years.</cite> Recovery dependent on China demand acceleration and Middle East de-escalation; valuations attractive for long-term but timing risk remains.
key_drivers: Middle East war geopolitical premium on oil/energy

tickers.thesis: Buffett's conglomerate benefits from stabilized market conditions and inflation-driven demand for insurance products. Portfolio heavy on tech (Apple, Amex exposure) provides upside if Fed accommodates; defensive positioning cushions downside if rates rise.
